visa_types.visa_fees:
[Table:Tourism ]
The above mentioned Visa Fee can be paid by Card / Cash (BHD only)
VFS SERVICE CHARGE 
In addition to the visa fees, applicants are subject to the payment of VFS Service charge of BHD 12.1 (Inclusive of 10% VAT).
Please Note:

- The Visa fee and service charges is non-refundable in case the visa is refused.
visa_types.processing_time:
Visa processing time can take up to 15 working days.
Kindly note that if you are applying from VFS in Bahrain, it will take additional 48 hours due to logistics.
visa_types.forms:
Visa application form has to be completed for all the applicants including minors electronically on your computer or in writing. The VAF has to be filled out in English. The form needs
                                                                        to be signed personally by the applicant in the field 37 and on the last page. In case of the applications for multiple-entry visas there has to be a signature also under the declaration
                                                                        about the necessity to have travel health insurance not only for the first trip but for all the following trips.
For minors, the application must be signed by one of the parents or a legal guardian.
Visa application form can be downloaded and printed from the link below. Forms are also available at the Visa Application Centre free of charge.
Kindly click here to download (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6d0zm94EX2xNSjafqAfhTy/1adb9483fb4c38bbd1070306f5e9c400/czech-republic-application-form-oct-2021.pdf)
visa_types.overview:
Overview
 The length of the visa validity shall correspond to the time necessary for the purpose of the transit.
 For list of countries, whose nationals are subject to transit visa in the Czech Republic and other information visit the website of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Czech Republic.
                                                                        Click here (http://www.mzv.cz/jnp/en/information_for_aliens/short_stay_visa/airport_visa.html) hyperlink: (http://www.mzv.cz/jnp/en/information_for_aliens/short_stay_visa/airport_visa.html)
 It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
 The length of the visa validity shall correspond to the time necessary for the purpose of the transit.
 For list of countries, whose nationals are subject to transit visa in the Czech Republic and other information visit the website of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Czech Republic.
                                                                        Click here (http://www.mzv.cz/jnp/en/information_for_aliens/short_stay_visa/airport_visa.html) hyperlink: (http://www.mzv.cz/jnp/en/information_for_aliens/short_stay_visa/airport_visa.html)
 It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience.

## Visa Fees

[Table:Tourism ]
The above mentioned Visa Fee can be paid by Card / Cash (BHD only)
VFS SERVICE CHARGE 
In addition to the visa fees, applicants are subject to the payment of VFS Service charge of BHD 12.1 (Inclusive of 10% VAT).
Please Note:
- The applicable visa fee in BHD is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
- The Visa fee and service charges is non-refundable in case the visa is refused.

## VISA Fee Exemption:
The following pay no fee: 
- Family members of a citizen of a Member State of the European Union or of a state which is bound by the Treaty on the European Economic Area or of a state bound by an international treaty with the European Communities, notwithstanding their nationality are exempt from the fee. The definition of a family member includes a spouse, direct descendants who are  less than 21 years old or who are dependent on the EU citizen, in certain cases also other family members (for verification kindly contact the Embassy).
- Persons under 6 years of age are exempt from the fee.
- Acceptance of an application to issue a visa in a diplomatic passport is exempt from the fee.
- Pupils, students, postgraduate students and accompanying teachers on journeys for the purpose of study or professional training in education are exempt from payment of fees.
- Researchers travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research are exempt from fees in accordance with the Recommendation No. 2005/761/ES of the European Parliament and the Council of 28 September 2005 to facilitate the issue of short-stay visas for researchers from third countries travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research.

## Documents Required

## Short Term Visa
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/bhr/en/cze/travel-insurance)
The length of the visa validity shall correspond to the time necessary for the purpose of the transit.
For list of countries, whose nationals are subject to transit visa in the Czech Republic and other information visit the website of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Czech Republic.   Click here (http://www.mzv.cz/jnp/en/information_for_aliens/short_stay_visa/airport_visa.html)
Airport Transit Checklist for Schengen visa application
Note regarding proof of sufficient financial means-
Proof of sufficient financial means covering the ticket reservation and pocket money (In case of stay no longer than 30 days minimum of 220 SAR per day and person, in case of two-month stay minimum of 8400 SAR per month and person, and in case of three month stay 9300 SAR per month per person) for the intended stay in the Schengen area:
- In Bahraini applicants original bank statement in English for the past 3 months is requested (Online print –out statements need to be stamped by the bank) or a copy of a credit card with the credit card statement
- In non-Bahraini applicants original bank statement in English for the past 6 months is requested (Online print –out statements need to be stamped by the bank). If the applicant does not receive his/her salary in the bank account, salary slips are requested.

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application for a visa should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up-to-date photograph (preferably maximum 6 months old)
- Must be a colour photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 x 4,5 cm
- Close up to your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph
- Full face taken straight forward 30 - 36 mm from point of the chin to head of hair
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed

## Processing Time
Visa processing time can take up to 15 working days.
Kindly note that if you are applying from VFS in Bahrain, it will take additional 48 hours due to logistics.

## Download Forms
Visa application form has to be completed for all the applicants including minors electronically on your computer or in writing. The VAF has to be filled out in English. The form needs
                                                                        to be signed personally by the applicant in the field 37 and on the last page. In case of the applications for multiple-entry visas there has to be a signature also under the declaration
                                                                        about the necessity to have travel health insurance not only for the first trip but for all the following trips.
For minors, the application must be signed by one of the parents or a legal guardian.
Visa application form can be downloaded and printed from the link below. Forms are also available at the Visa Application Centre free of charge.
Kindly click here to download (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6d0zm94EX2xNSjafqAfhTy/1adb9483fb4c38bbd1070306f5e9c400/czech-republic-application-form-oct-2021.pdf)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Applicable for the purpose of attending a business meeting, a conference or a short professional training in the Czech Republic. 
The Schengen visa may be issued for a single entry (as a rule for the 1st time visitors of the Schengen area), for two entries, or as a multiple entry visa in case the applicant proves
                                                                        the need or justifies the intention to travel frequently as well as proves his/her integrity and reliability, in particular the lawful use of previous Schengen visas, etc. (as per
                                                                        Schengen Visa Code, Article 24). A non-EU citizen traveller, regardless of the fact whether he/she is subject to visa obligation, may stay in the Schengen area for a maximum of 90
                                                                        days in any 180-day period. 
It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience. For verification if the Embassy of the Czech Republic is the right consulate to examine and decide your
                                                                        visa application, please see the Short Term Visa section. Click here
It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Applicable for the purpose of attending a business meeting, a conference or a short professional training in the Czech Republic. 
The Schengen visa may be issued for a single entry (as a rule for the 1st time visitors of the Schengen area), for two entries, or as a multiple entry visa in case the applicant proves
                                                                        the need or justifies the intention to travel frequently as well as proves his/her integrity and reliability, in particular the lawful use of previous Schengen visas, etc. (as per
                                                                        Schengen Visa Code, Article 24). A non-EU citizen traveller, regardless of the fact whether he/she is subject to visa obligation, may stay in the Schengen area for a maximum of 90
                                                                        days in any 180-day period. 
It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience. For verification if the Embassy of the Czech Republic is the right consulate to examine and decide your
                                                                        visa application, please see the Short Term Visa section. Click here
It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience.

## Visa Fees

[Table:Business]
The above mentioned Visa Fee can be paid by Card / Cash (BHD only)
In addition to the visa fees, applicants are subject to the payment of VFS Service charge of BHD 12.1 (Inclusive of 10% VAT).
Please Note:
- The applicable visa fee in BHD is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
- The Visa fee and service charges is non-refundable in case the visa is refused.

## VISA Fee Exemption:

The following pay no fee: 
- Family members of a citizen of a Member State of the European Union or of a state which is bound by the Treaty on the European Economic Area or of a state bound by an international  treaty with the European Communities, notwithstanding their nationality are exempt from the fee. The definition of a family member includes a spouse, direct descendants who are less than 21 years old or who are dependent on the EU citizen, in certain cases also other family members (for verification kindly contact the Embassy).
- Persons under 6 years of age are exempt from the fee.
- Acceptance of an application to issue a visa in a diplomatic passport is exempt from the fee.
- Pupils, students, postgraduate students and accompanying teachers on journeys for the purpose of study or professional training in education are exempt from payment of fees.
- Researchers travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research are exempt from fees in accordance with the Recommendation No. 2005/761/ES of the European  Parliament and the Council of 28 September 2005 to facilitate the issue of short-stay visas for researchers from third countries travelling within the Community for the purpose  of carrying out scientific research.

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/bhr/en/cze/travel-insurance).

visa_types.overview:
Overview
- Tourism
- Applicable for solely tourism purposes. If tourism is joined with another purpose such as a business meeting, visit of relatives/wedding, cultural event, etc., such other purpose
                                                                            should be declared as the main one.
- The Schengen visa may be issued for a single entry (as a rule for the 1st time visitors of the Schengen area), for two entries, or as a multiple entry visa in case the applicant
proves the need or justifies the       intention to travel frequently
as well as proves his/her integrity and reliability, in particular the lawful use of previous Schengen visas, etc. (as per Schengen Visa Code, Article 24). A non-EU citizen traveller,
                                                                            regardless of the fact whether he/she is subject to visa obligation, may stay in the Schengen area for a maximum of 90 days in any 180-day period.
It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience. For verification if the Embassy of the Czech Republic is the right consulate to examine and decide your
                                                                        visa application, please see the Short Term Visa section: click here

- Tourism
- Applicable for solely tourism purposes. If tourism is joined with another purpose such as a business meeting, visit of relatives/wedding, cultural event, etc., such other purpose
                                                                            should be declared as the main one.
- The Schengen visa may be issued for a single entry (as a rule for the 1st time visitors of the Schengen area), for two entries, or as a multiple entry visa in case the applicant
proves the need or justifies the       intention to travel frequently
as well as proves his/her integrity and reliability, in particular the lawful use of previous Schengen visas, etc. (as per Schengen Visa Code, Article 24). A non-EU citizen traveller,
                                                                            regardless of the fact whether he/she is subject to visa obligation, may stay in the Schengen area for a maximum of 90 days in any 180-day period.
It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience. For verification if the Embassy of the Czech Republic is the right consulate to examine and decide your
                                                                        visa application, please see the Short Term Visa section: click here

visa_types.overview:
Overview
 Applicable for the purpose of visiting a family member or a friend. 
In case of staying with family or friends in the Czech Republic, outside the commercial accommodation facility, the foreigner is obliged to report within 3 working days after the
                                                                            arrival in the Czech Republic to the respective local immigration police authority.
The Schengen visa may be issued for a single entry (as a rule for the 1st time visitors of the Schengen area), for two entries, or as a multiple entry visa in case the applicant proves
                                                                            the need or justifies the intention to travel frequently as well as proves his/her integrity and reliability, in particular the lawful use of previous Schengen visas, etc. (as
                                                                            per Schengen Visa Code, Article 24). A non-EU citizen traveller, regardless of the fact whether he/she is subject to visa obligation, may stay in the Schengen area for a maximum
                                                                            of 90 days in any 180-day period.
It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
 Applicable for the purpose of visiting a family member or a friend. 
In case of staying with family or friends in the Czech Republic, outside the commercial accommodation facility, the foreigner is obliged to report within 3 working days after the
                                                                            arrival in the Czech Republic to the respective local immigration police authority.
The Schengen visa may be issued for a single entry (as a rule for the 1st time visitors of the Schengen area), for two entries, or as a multiple entry visa in case the applicant proves
                                                                            the need or justifies the intention to travel frequently as well as proves his/her integrity and reliability, in particular the lawful use of previous Schengen visas, etc. (as
                                                                            per Schengen Visa Code, Article 24). A non-EU citizen traveller, regardless of the fact whether he/she is subject to visa obligation, may stay in the Schengen area for a maximum
                                                                            of 90 days in any 180-day period.
It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience.

## Visa Fees

[Table:Visit To Family or Friends]
The above mentioned Visa Fee can be paid by Card / Cash (BHD only)
VFS SERVICE CHARGE 
In addition to the visa fees, applicants are subject to the payment of VFS Service charge of BHD 12.1 (Inclusive of 10% VAT).
Please Note:
- The applicable visa fee in BHD is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
- The Visa fee and service charges is non-refundable in case the visa is refused.

## VISA Fee Exemption:
The following pay no fee: 
- Family members of a citizen of a Member State of the European Union or of a state which is bound by the Treaty on the European Economic Area or of a state bound by an international treaty with the European Communities, notwithstanding their nationality are exempt from the fee. The definition of a family member includes a spouse, direct descendants who are  less than 21 years old or who are dependent on the EU citizen, in certain cases also other family members (for verification kindly contact the Embassy).
- Persons under 6 years of age are exempt from the fee.
- Acceptance of an application to issue a visa in a diplomatic passport is exempt from the fee.
- Pupils, students, postgraduate students and accompanying teachers on journeys for the purpose of study or professional training in education are exempt from payment of fees.
- Researchers travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research are exempt from fees in accordance with the Recommendation No. 2005/761/ES of the European Parliament and the Council of 28 September 2005 to facilitate the issue of short-stay visas for researchers from third countries travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research.

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/bhr/en/cze/travel-insurance).

Checklist EUcitizen2026

Checklist VFS BHR 2026_Bahraini nationals_sanitized
Checklist VFS BHR 2026_Non-Bahraini nationals_sanitized
Sponsorship Letter (https://assets.ctfassets.net/bhgpgbxkktod/45PymuDvrBOpCePDMPmpmR/ac22f25fc39c3d95b95f4bf859610276/affidavit-on-sponsorship.pdf)
Travel Insurance
Note regarding proof of sufficient financial means-
Proof of sufficient financial means covering the ticket reservation and pocket money (In case of stay no longer than 30 days minimum of 220 SAR per day and person, in case of two-month stay minimum of 8400 SAR per month and person, and in case of three month stay 9300 SAR per month per person) for the intended stay in the Schengen area:
- In Bahraini applicants original bank statement in English for the past 3 months is requested (Online print –out statements need to be stamped by the bank) or a copy of a credit card with the credit card statement
- In non-Bahraini applicants original bank statement in English for the past 6 months is requested (Online print –out statements need to be stamped by the bank). If the applicant does not receive his/her salary in the bank account, salary slips are requested.

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application for a visa should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up-to-date photograph (preferably maximum 6 months old)
- Must be a colour photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 x 4,5 cm
- Close up to your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph
- Full face taken straight forward 30 - 36 mm from point of the chin to head of hair
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed

## Processing Time
Visa processing time can take up to 15 working days.
Kindly note that if you are applying from VFS in Bahrain, it will take additional 48 hours due to logistics.

## Download Forms
Visa application form has to be completed for all the applicants including minors electronically on your computer or in writing. The VAF has to be filled out in English. The form needs
                                                                        to be signed personally by the applicant in the field 37 and on the last page. In case of the applications for multiple-entry visas there has to be a signature also under the declaration
                                                                        about the necessity to have travel health insurance not only for the first trip but for all the following trips.
For minors, the application must be signed by one of the parents or a legal guardian.
Visa application form can be downloaded and printed from the link below. Forms are also available at the Visa Application Centre free of charge.
Kindly click here to download (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6d0zm94EX2xNSjafqAfhTy/1adb9483fb4c38bbd1070306f5e9c400/czech-republic-application-form-oct-2021.pdf)

visa_types.name: Others
visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/bhr/en/cze/travel-insurance).
Checklist EUcitizen2026

Checklist VFS BHR 2026_Bahraini nationals_sanitized
Checklist VFS BHR 2026_Non-Bahraini nationals_sanitized
Sponsorship Letter (https://assets.ctfassets.net/bhgpgbxkktod/45PymuDvrBOpCePDMPmpmR/ac22f25fc39c3d95b95f4bf859610276/affidavit-on-sponsorship.pdf)
Travel Insurance
Documents to be provided by all visa applicants 
- Visa Application Form correctly filled in English, dated and signed by the applicant (available online, free of charge, at the website of
the Ministry of  Foreign Affairs of the Czech Republic (http://www.mzv.cz/)
or the website of
the Embassy of the Czech Republic in Riyadh (http://www.mzv.cz/riyadh/en/visas_and_consular_information/visas/schengen_visa_requirements/index.html)
- One recent passport-sized photograph with a white background, not stapled. Full face; no head or face cover(s) that prevent(s) full visibility of the facial features. No head covering except for religious reasons.
- Original passport valid for further three months period after the intended departure from the territory of the Schengen States and 1 copy of all relevant passport pages (data page, pages containing previous Schengen visas). The passport must have been issued in the previous 10 years and contain at least two blank pages. (Remark: if a passport does not state place of birth, ID card needs to be presented.)
- Proof of travel medical insurance covering the entire period of intended stay, valid for all Schengen States and covering any expenses which might arise in connection with repatriation for medical reasons, urgent medical attention, emergency hospital treatment or death during stay. The minimum coverage must be 30.000 Euros. Original and 1 copy thereof.
- Copy of flight ticket or flight reservation, including return flight.
- Verifiable evidence of sufficient means of subsistence during intended stay such as a credit card valid throughout the intended stay.
- For minors travelling without their parents/legal guardian: original authorization signed by their parents/legal guardian and copy of passport(s) of parents/legal guardian.
Additionally, for Non-Bahraini Nationals 
- Iqama and 1 copy thereof.
- Bahrain re-entry visa valid beyond the intended date of departure from the territory of the Schengen States and 1 copy thereof.
- Bank statements for the past six months or other verifiable evidence of sufficient means of subsistence during intended stay.
Proof of sufficient financial means covering the ticket reservation and pocket money (In case of stay no longer than 30 days minimum of 220 SAR per day and person, in case of two-month stay minimum of 8400 SAR per month and person, and in case of three month stay 9300 SAR per month per person) for the intended stay in the Schengen area:
- In Bahraini applicants original bank statement in English for the past 3 months is requested (Online print –out statements need to be stamped by the bank) or a copy of a credit card with the credit card statement
- In non-Bahraini applicants original bank statement in English for the past 6 months is requested (Online print –out statements need to be stamped by the bank). If the applicant does not receive his/her salary in the bank account, salary slips are requested.
- Original letter from sponsor in Bahrain, stating employment status, position, length ofcontract and salary, stamped by the Chamber of Commerce. For Self-sponsored: valid original trade licence and 1 copy thereof with translation. For domestic staff: original employment contract with the sponsor + 1 copy thereof, original Non Objection Letter/Guarantee Letter from the sponsor accepting full responsibility (housemaids can travel only together with the sponsor, a prerequisite is the duration of employment at least one year before application for visa).
A. Visa for Business Travel Invitation letter from a company or an authority to attend a meeting, conference or event or other documents which show the existence of trade relations or relations for work purposes (e.g. confirmed registrations for trade fairs or congresses), including a proof of accommodation during the intended stay.
B. Visa for Tourism or for Private Visits For tourism: Proof of accommodation during intended stay: hotel reservation or copy of a rental contract or proof of residential property.   For private visits: Original invitation letter signed by the host covering the intended stay [where applicable: using the official format required by the Member State's national legislation.  For further information, please verify the website of the Member State(s) of destination]. C. Visa for Medical Treatment A document issued by the medical institution in the Schengen Member State confirming that the applicant will receive medical care in that institution. Pre-payment or other proof of sufficient financial means to cover the medical treatment and related expenses, such as insurance coverage.
D. Visa for Study or Training Certificate of enrolment at an educational establishment in the Schengen Member State for the purposes of attending vocational or theoretical courses
                                                                        within the framework of basic and further training or student cards or certificates of the courses to be attended.
E. Diplomatic and other Official Government Missions Verbal Note from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Kingdom of Bahrain. For Non-Bahraini Nationals: Verbal Note from their Ministry of Foreign Affairs or Diplomatic Mission in Bahrain.
F. Airport Transit Visa Valid visa for the country of next or final destination.
Proof of the intention of carrying out the onward journey: copy of continuation ticket or reservation.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Applicable for solely medical treatments in the Czech Republic. In case of wellness programmes tourism purpose should be declared.
Patients and their accompanying persons who plan to undergo a medical treatment longer than 90 days in the Czech spas may apply for a long-term visa via an accelerated procedure that
                                                                        takes maximum 30 days. The procedure needs to be initiated through the respective spa management and finalized before the trip. Participating spas: Lazne Darkov, Lecebne lazne Jachymov,
                                                                        Thermal-F, Lazne Teplice v Cechach, Lecebne lazne Lazne Kynzvart, Statni lecebne lazne Janske lazne, Lazne Podebrady, and Lazne Belohrad.
The Schengen visa may be issued for a single entry (as a rule for the 1st time visitors of the Schengen area), for two entries, or as a multiple entry visa in case the applicant proves
                                                                        the need or justifies the intention to travel frequently as well as proves his/her integrity and reliability, in particular the lawful use of previous Schengen visas, etc. (as per
                                                                        Schengen Visa Code, Article 24). A non-EU citizen traveller, regardless of the fact whether he/she is subject to visa obligation, may stay in the Schengen area for a maximum of 90
                                                                        days in any 180-day period.
                                                                    
It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Applicable for solely medical treatments in the Czech Republic. In case of wellness programmes tourism purpose should be declared.
Patients and their accompanying persons who plan to undergo a medical treatment longer than 90 days in the Czech spas may apply for a long-term visa via an accelerated procedure that
                                                                        takes maximum 30 days. The procedure needs to be initiated through the respective spa management and finalized before the trip. Participating spas: Lazne Darkov, Lecebne lazne Jachymov,
                                                                        Thermal-F, Lazne Teplice v Cechach, Lecebne lazne Lazne Kynzvart, Statni lecebne lazne Janske lazne, Lazne Podebrady, and Lazne Belohrad.
The Schengen visa may be issued for a single entry (as a rule for the 1st time visitors of the Schengen area), for two entries, or as a multiple entry visa in case the applicant proves
                                                                        the need or justifies the intention to travel frequently as well as proves his/her integrity and reliability, in particular the lawful use of previous Schengen visas, etc. (as per
                                                                        Schengen Visa Code, Article 24). A non-EU citizen traveller, regardless of the fact whether he/she is subject to visa obligation, may stay in the Schengen area for a maximum of 90
                                                                        days in any 180-day period.
                                                                    
It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience.

- Pupils, students, postgraduate students and accompanying teachers on journeys for the purpose of study or professional training in education are exempt from payment of fees.

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application for a visa should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up-to-date photograph (preferably maximum 6 months old)
- Must be a colour photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 x 4,5 cm
- Close up to your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph
- Full face taken straight forward 30 - 36 mm from point of the chin to head of hair
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed

## Processing Time
Visa processing time can take up to 15 working days.
Kindly note that if you are applying from VFS in Bahrain, it will take additional 48 hours due to logistics.

## Download Forms
Visa application form has to be completed for all the applicants including minors electronically on your computer or in writing. The VAF has to be filled out in English. The form needs
                                                                        to be signed personally by the applicant in the field 37 and on the last page. In case of the applications for multiple-entry visas there has to be a signature also under the declaration
                                                                        about the necessity to have travel health insurance not only for the first trip but for all the following trips.
For minors, the application must be signed by one of the parents or a legal guardian.
Visa application form can be downloaded and printed from the link below. Forms are also available at the Visa Application Centre free of charge.
Kindly click here to download (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6d0zm94EX2xNSjafqAfhTy/1adb9483fb4c38bbd1070306f5e9c400/czech-republic-application-form-oct-2021.pdf)

visa_types.name: Long stay visa and residence permit
visa_types.overview:
Applications for long-term visas and residence permits (for stays longer than 90 days) need to be submitted directly at the Embassy of the Czech Republic in Riyadh. For appointment kindly contact the embassy at the following website: riyadh.consulate@mzv.gov.cz  (emailto:riyadh.consulate@mzv.gov.cz)
For information on long-term visa types and requirements kindly consult the website of the Ministry of Interior of the Czech Republic. Click Here (https://ipc.gov.cz/en/) or the website of the Embassy of the Czech Republic in Riyadh. Click Here (https://mzv.gov.cz/riyadh/en/visas_and_consular_information/index.html)

VFS SERVICE CHARGE 
In addition to the visa fees, applicants are subject to the payment of VFS Service charge of BHD 12.1 (Inclusive of 10% VAT).
Please Note:

visa_types.processing_time:

Kindly note that if you are applying from VFS in Bahrain , Manama , it will take additional 48 hours due to logistics
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Within the Schengen visa framework, a family member of an EU citizen is a family member of a citizen of any of the EU country or Iceland, Lichtenstein, Norway and Switzerland, who is a spouse or a direct descendant less than 21 years old or dependent on the EU citizen. In certain cases, also other relatives can be regarded as “family members“(for verification kindly contact the Embassy).
The Schengen visa may be issued for a single entry (as a rule for the 1st time visitors of the Schengen area), for two entries, or as a multiple entry visa in case the applicant proves the need or justifies the intention to travel frequently as well as proves his/her integrity and reliability, in particular the lawful use of previous Schengen visas, etc. (as per Schengen Visa Code, Article 24). A non-EU citizen traveller, regardless of the fact whether he/she is subject to visa obligation, may stay in the Schengen area for a maximum of 90 days within any 180-day period.
It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Within the Schengen visa framework, a family member of an EU citizen is a family member of a citizen of any of the EU country or Iceland, Lichtenstein, Norway and Switzerland, who is a spouse or a direct descendant less than 21 years old or dependent on the EU citizen. In certain cases, also other relatives can be regarded as “family members“(for verification kindly contact the Embassy).
The Schengen visa may be issued for a single entry (as a rule for the 1st time visitors of the Schengen area), for two entries, or as a multiple entry visa in case the applicant proves the need or justifies the intention to travel frequently as well as proves his/her integrity and reliability, in particular the lawful use of previous Schengen visas, etc. (as per Schengen Visa Code, Article 24). A non-EU citizen traveller, regardless of the fact whether he/she is subject to visa obligation, may stay in the Schengen area for a maximum of 90 days within any 180-day period.
It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience. 

## Visa Fees

[Table:Tourism ]
The above mentioned Visa Fee can be paid by Card / Cash (BHD only)
VFS SERVICE CHARGE 
In addition to the visa fees, applicants are subject to the payment of VFS Service charge of BHD 12.1 (Inclusive of 10% VAT).
Please Note:
- The applicable visa fee in BHD is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
- The Visa fee and service charges is non-refundable in case the visa is refused.

## VISA Fee Exemption:
The following pay no fee: 
- Family members of a citizen of a Member State of the European Union or of a state which is bound by the Treaty on the European Economic Area or of a state bound by an international treaty with the European Communities, notwithstanding their nationality are exempt from the fee. The definition of a family member includes a spouse, direct descendants who are  less than 21 years old or who are dependent on the EU citizen, in certain cases also other family members (for verification kindly contact the Embassy).
- Persons under 6 years of age are exempt from the fee.
- Acceptance of an application to issue a visa in a diplomatic passport is exempt from the fee.
- Pupils, students, postgraduate students and accompanying teachers on journeys for the purpose of study or professional training in education are exempt from payment of fees.
- Researchers travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research are exempt from fees in accordance with the Recommendation No. 2005/761/ES of the European Parliament and the Council of 28 September 2005 to facilitate the issue of short-stay visas for researchers from third countries travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research.

## Documents Required
The length of the visa validity shall correspond to the time necessary for the purpose of the EU citizen family member.
For list of countries, whose nationals are subject to transit visa in the Czech Republic and other information visit the website of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Czech Republic (https://mzv.gov.cz/jnp/en/information_for_aliens/index.html).
Checklist

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application for a visa should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up-to-date photograph (preferably maximum 6 months old)
- Must be a colour photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 x 4,5 cm
- Close up to your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph
- Full face taken straight forward 30 - 36 mm from point of the chin to head of hair
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed

## Processing Time
Visa processing time can take up to 15 working days.
Kindly note that if you are applying from VFS in Bahrain , Manama , it will take additional 48 hours due to logistics
